mn151:1.2: at one time the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
mn151:1.3: Then Venerable Sāriputta, emerging from retreat in the evening, went to the *Bhagavā*; having gone, he paid homage to the *Bhagavā* and sat to one side. As Venerable Sāriputta was sitting to one side, the *Bhagavā* said this to him:
mn151:10.1: Furthermore, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* should reflect in this way:
mn151:10.2: ‘Have the five hindrances been abandoned in me?’
mn151:10.3: If, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, reflecting, knows thus:
mn151:10.4: ‘The five hindrances have not been abandoned in me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should strive for the abandoning of the five hindrances.
mn151:10.5: But if, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, reflecting, knows thus:
mn151:10.6: ‘The five hindrances have been abandoned in me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should dwell with that very *pīti* and gladness [*pītipāmojjena*], training day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
mn151:11.1: Furthermore, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* should reflect in this way:
mn151:11.2: ‘Have the five aggregates of *upādāna* [*pañcupādānakkhandhā*] been fully understood in me?’
mn151:11.3: If, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, reflecting, knows thus:
mn151:11.4: ‘The five aggregates of *upādāna* [*pañcupādānakkhandhā*] have not been fully understood in me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should strive for the full understanding of the five aggregates of *upādāna*.
mn151:11.5: But if, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, reflecting, knows thus:
mn151:11.6: ‘The five aggregates of *upādāna* [*pañcupādānakkhandhā*] have been fully understood in me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should dwell with that very *pīti* and gladness [*pītipāmojjena*], training day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
mn151:12.1: Furthermore, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* should reflect in this way:
mn151:12.2: ‘Have the four *satipaṭṭhānas* been developed [*bhāvitā*] in me?’
mn151:12.3: If, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, reflecting, knows thus:
mn151:12.4: ‘The four *satipaṭṭhānas* have not been developed [*abhāvitā*] in me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should strive for the development [*bhāvanāya*] of the four *satipaṭṭhānas*.
mn151:12.5: But if, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, reflecting, knows thus:
mn151:12.6: ‘The four *satipaṭṭhānas* have been developed [*bhāvitā*] in me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should dwell with that very *pīti* and gladness [*pītipāmojjena*], training day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
mn151:13.1: Furthermore, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* should reflect in this way:
mn151:13.2: ‘Have the four right efforts been developed [*bhāvitā*] in me?’
mn151:13.3: If, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, reflecting, knows thus:
mn151:13.4: ‘The four right efforts have not been developed [*abhāvitā*] in me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should strive for the development [*bhāvanāya*] of the four right efforts.
mn151:13.5: But if, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, reflecting, knows thus:
mn151:13.6: ‘The four right efforts have been developed [*bhāvitā*] in me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should dwell with that very *pīti* and gladness [*pītipāmojjena*], training day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
mn151:14.1: Furthermore, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* should reflect in this way:
mn151:14.2: ‘Have the four bases of spiritual power been developed [*bhāvitā*] in me?’
mn151:14.3: If, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, reflecting, knows thus:
mn151:14.4: ‘The four bases of spiritual power have not been developed [*abhāvitā*] in me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should strive for the development [*bhāvanāya*] of the four bases of spiritual power.
mn151:14.5: But if, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, reflecting, knows thus:
mn151:14.6: ‘The four bases of spiritual power have been developed [*bhāvitā*] in me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should dwell with that very *pīti* and gladness [*pītipāmojjena*], training day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
mn151:15.1: Furthermore, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* should reflect in this way:
mn151:15.2: ‘Have the five *indriyas* been developed [*bhāvitāni*] in me?’
mn151:15.3: If, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, reflecting, knows thus:
mn151:15.4: ‘The five *indriyas* have not been developed [*abhāvitāni*] in me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should strive for the development [*bhāvanāya*] of the five *indriyas*.
mn151:15.5: But if, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, reflecting, knows thus:
mn151:15.6: ‘The five *indriyas* have been developed [*bhāvitāni*] in me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should dwell with that very *pīti* and gladness [*pītipāmojjena*], training day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
mn151:16.1: Furthermore, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* should reflect in this way:
mn151:16.2: ‘Have the five *balas* been developed [*bhāvitāni*] in me?’
mn151:16.3: If, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, reflecting, knows thus:
mn151:16.4: ‘The five *balas* have not been developed [*abhāvitāni*] in me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should strive for the development [*bhāvanāya*] of the five *balas*.
mn151:16.5: But if, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, reflecting, knows thus:
mn151:16.6: ‘The five *balas* have been developed [*bhāvitāni*] in me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should dwell with that very *pīti* and gladness [*pītipāmojjena*], training day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
mn151:17.1: Furthermore, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* should reflect in this way:
mn151:17.2: ‘Have the seven *bojjhaṅgas* been developed [*bhāvitā*] in me?’
mn151:17.3: If, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, reflecting, knows thus:
mn151:17.4: ‘The seven *bojjhaṅgas* have not been developed [*abhāvitā*] in me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should strive for the development [*bhāvanāya*] of the seven *bojjhaṅgas*.
mn151:17.5: But if, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, reflecting, knows thus:
mn151:17.6: ‘The seven *bojjhaṅgas* have been developed [*bhāvitā*] in me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should dwell with that very *pīti* and gladness [*pītipāmojjena*], training day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
mn151:18.1: Furthermore, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* should reflect in this way:
mn151:18.2: ‘Has the noble eightfold path been developed [*bhāvito*] in me?’
mn151:18.3: If, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, reflecting, knows thus:
mn151:18.4: ‘The noble eightfold path has not been developed [*abhāvito*] in me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should strive for the development [*bhāvanāya*] of the noble eightfold path.
mn151:18.5: But if, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, reflecting, knows thus:
mn151:18.6: ‘The noble eightfold path has been developed [*bhāvito*] in me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should dwell with that very *pīti* and gladness [*pītipāmojjena*], training day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
mn151:19.1: Furthermore, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* should reflect in this way:
mn151:19.2: ‘Have settling [*samatho*] and *vipassanā* been developed [*bhāvitā*] in me?’
mn151:19.3: If, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, reflecting, knows thus:
mn151:19.4: ‘Settling [*samatho*] and *vipassanā* have not been developed [*abhāvitā*] in me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should strive for the development [*bhāvanāya*] of settling [*samatha*] and *vipassanā*.
mn151:19.5: But if, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, reflecting, knows thus:
mn151:19.6: ‘Settling [*samatho*] and *vipassanā* have been developed [*bhāvitā*] in me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should dwell with that very *pīti* and gladness [*pītipāmojjena*], training day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
mn151:2.1: “Your *indriyas* are serene, Sāriputta; the color of your skin is pure and bright.
mn151:2.2: In what dwelling, Sāriputta, do you now dwell much?”
mn151:2.3: “In the dwelling of emptiness, *bhante*, I now dwell much.”
mn151:2.5: In the dwelling of a great person, it seems, Sāriputta, you now dwell much.
mn151:2.6: This is the dwelling of a great person, Sāriputta, namely—
mn151:20.1: Furthermore, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* should reflect in this way:
mn151:20.2: ‘Have knowledge and liberation been realized in me?’
mn151:20.3: If, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, reflecting, knows thus:
mn151:20.4: ‘Knowledge and liberation have not been realized in me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should strive for the realization of knowledge and liberation.
mn151:20.5: But if, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, reflecting, knows thus:
mn151:20.6: ‘Knowledge and liberation have been realized in me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should dwell with that very *pīti* and gladness [*pītipāmojjena*], training day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
mn151:21.1: For whatever ascetics or brahmins in the past, Sāriputta, purified almsfood, all of them purified almsfood by reflecting and reflecting in just this way.
mn151:21.2: And whatever ascetics or brahmins in the future, Sāriputta, will purify almsfood, all of them will purify almsfood by reflecting and reflecting in just this way.
mn151:21.3: And whatever ascetics or brahmins at present, Sāriputta, purify almsfood, all of them purify almsfood by reflecting and reflecting in just this way.
mn151:21.4: Therefore, Sāriputta, ‘We will purify almsfood by reflecting and reflecting’—
mn151:21.5: that is how you should train, Sāriputta.”
mn151:21.7: Satisfied, Venerable Sāriputta rejoiced in what the *Bhagavā* had said.
mn151:21.8: The *sutta* on Purification of Almsfood is finished, the ninth.
mn151:3.1: Therefore, Sāriputta, if a *bhikkhu* should wish:
mn151:3.2: ‘May I dwell much in the dwelling of emptiness,’ that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should reflect in this way:
mn151:3.3: ‘On the road by which I entered the village for alms, in the place where I walked for alms, and on the road by which I returned from the village after alms, is there in me there any desire, lust, hate, delusion, or mental resistance toward forms [*rūpesu*] cognizable by the eye?’
mn151:3.4: If, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, reflecting, knows thus:
mn151:3.5: ‘On the road by which I entered the village for alms, in the place where I walked for alms, and on the road by which I returned from the village after alms, there is in me there desire, lust, hate, delusion, or mental resistance toward forms [*rūpesu*] cognizable by the eye,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should strive for the abandoning of those very bad, unskillful *dhammas*.
mn151:3.6: But if, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, reflecting, knows thus:
mn151:3.7: ‘On the road by which I entered the village for alms, in the place where I walked for alms, and on the road by which I returned from the village after alms, there is not in me there desire, lust, hate, delusion, or mental resistance toward forms [*rūpesu*] cognizable by the eye,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should dwell with that very *pīti* and gladness [*pītipāmojjena*], training day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
mn151:4-8.1: Furthermore, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* should reflect in this way:
mn151:4-8.10: ‘On the road by which I entered the village for alms, in the place where I walked for alms, and on the road by which I returned from the village after alms, there is not in me there desire, lust, hate, delusion, or mental resistance toward *dhammas* cognizable by the mind,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should dwell with that very *pīti* and gladness [*pītipāmojjena*], training day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
mn151:4-8.2: ‘On the road by which I entered the village for alms, in the place where I walked for alms, and on the road by which I returned from the village after alms, is there in me there any desire, lust, hate, delusion, or mental resistance toward sounds cognizable by the ear …
mn151:4-8.3: toward odors cognizable by the nose …
mn151:4-8.4: toward tastes cognizable by the tongue …
mn151:4-8.5: toward tangibles cognizable by the body …
mn151:4-8.7: If, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, reflecting, knows thus:
mn151:4-8.8: ‘On the road by which I entered the village for alms, in the place where I walked for alms, and on the road by which I returned from the village after alms, there is in me there desire, lust, hate, delusion, or mental resistance toward *dhammas* cognizable by the mind,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should strive for the abandoning of those very bad, unskillful *dhammas*.
mn151:4-8.9: But if, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, reflecting, knows thus:
mn151:9.1: Furthermore, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu* should reflect in this way:
mn151:9.2: ‘Have the five strands of sensuality been abandoned in me?’
mn151:9.3: If, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, reflecting, knows thus:
mn151:9.4: ‘The five strands of sensuality have not been abandoned in me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should strive for the abandoning of the five strands of sensuality.
mn151:9.5: But if, Sāriputta, a *bhikkhu*, reflecting, knows thus:
mn151:9.6: ‘The five strands of sensuality have been abandoned in me,’ then that *bhikkhu*, Sāriputta, should dwell with that very *pīti* and gladness [*pītipāmojjena*], training day and night in skillful *dhammas*.
